$comment: Config for scripts/token-cost/token-cost.mjs. All costs are estimates unless a commit carries a Token-Usage trailer — see docs/token-cost-tracking.md for what the numbers are based on and how to calibrate them.
$billingMode: api = multiply tokens by per-MTok prices below. subscription = flat-rate plan (Claude Pro/Max, ChatGPT Plus, etc.); costs are still computed and shown at API rates (the API-equivalent of the usage), labelled api-equiv since the plan's marginal cost is $0.

gpt-5.2.$note: EXAMPLE — verify at openai.com/api/pricing before relying on this

$models: Per-MTok USD prices. Claude prices verified 2026-07 from Anthropic docs. Non-Anthropic entries are placeholders — update them from the provider's pricing page. Add any model your team uses; the key must match the model name used in Token-Usage trailers.

cache.$comment: Cache-read tokens are billed at ~0.1x the input rate, cache writes at ~1.25x (5-min TTL; 1h TTL is 2x). Applied per model when computing cost from captured session usage.

capture.$comment: Where Claude Code writes session transcripts. `record` reads the per-response usage from this project's transcript files (a purely local read — costs nothing) and attaches it to the commit as a git note on refs/notes/token-usage. The session currently active (or active within staleAfterMinutes) is attributed to the commit automatically; for any other session in the capture window you are asked whether it was related to this work (default Y; declined usage is dropped, not deferred). Prompts need a terminal — in CI/GUI clients everything is included, as before. Set promptForPreviousSessions to false to never ask. For other AI tools, use `capture --input N --output N --model X` instead.

estimation.$comment: Used only when a commit has no Token-Usage trailer. outputTokens = addedLines * charsPerLine / charsPerToken. inputTokens = outputTokens * inputMultiplier (coding agents read far more context than they write). Calibrate: compare a week of estimates against real usage from `/cost` in Claude Code or your provider's usage dashboard, then adjust these three numbers.

$excludePaths: Regex list. Diff lines in matching files are ignored by the estimator (lockfiles and assets are not written token-by-token by a model).
